title: pyrus aria var. flabellifolia
intro: Pyrus aria var. flabellifolia, also known as Mountain Ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its white flowers and red berries. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Pyrus aria var. flabellifolia include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions and promote overall well-being.
